markets.question: Israel x Hezbollah diplomatic meeting by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of Israel and Hezbollah by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of Israel and Hezbollah who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding Israel-Hezbollah relations on behalf of their governments/leadership structures. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the government of Israel and Hezbollah leadership, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid fragile US-brokered ceasefires starting April 16 and extended April 23 between Israel and Lebanon to halt Israel-Hezbollah hostilities, recent escalations—including Israel's May 6 airstrike on Beirut suburbs killing a Hezbollah Radwan Forces commander—have heightened tensions and US accusations that Hezbollah seeks to derail talks. While Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors held prior mediated rounds in Washington with White House involvement under Trump, resulting in walkouts and concurrent Hezbollah rocket fire, no direct Israel-Hezbollah diplomatic engagement has materialized, as Hezbollah signals openness only to indirect negotiations. New ambassador-level talks are scheduled for May 14-15 in Washington to discuss disarmament and borders, potentially influencing de-escalation prospects before the market's key dates.

markets.question: Will New Slovenia – Christian Democrats (NSi) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 22, 2026, parliamentary elections under proportional representation, no party secured a majority in the 90-seat National Assembly, with Prime Minister Robert Golob's liberal Freedom Movement taking 29 seats and Janez Janša's right-wing Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) 28 seats. Golob's bid for a broad coalition collapsed on April 20 amid rejections from center-right groups, prompting President Nataša Pirc Musar to decline nominating a prime minister-designate on April 25 due to insufficient pledged support. Recent developments, including a May 5 announcement by Janša of agreed coalition guidelines and a prior parliamentary vote passing a government reform with 49 votes, signal advancing talks for a minority center-right cabinet potentially involving SDS, NSi-SLS-Fokus alliance (9 seats), Democrats (6 seats), and Resni.ca (5 seats) external support—needing 46 votes for investiture. Traders monitor imminent National Assembly votes amid deadlock risks or snap election calls.

markets.question: Will Natural Gas (NG) hit (LOW) $2.00 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of Natural Gas futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For Natural Gas futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For Natural Gas (NG) futures, the last trading day is defined as the third last business day of the month prior to the contract month, consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month Natural Gas futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=NGD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month Natural Gas (NG) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Natural Gas (NG) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Natural Gas (NG) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Henry Hub natural gas spot prices climbed to $2.80 per MMBtu on May 8, 2026, reflecting trader consensus on a smaller-than-expected 63 Bcf storage injection for the week ending May 1—below forecasts of 72 Bcf—and recent production dips alongside near-record LNG exports. Inventories stand at 2,205 Bcf, building modestly in the shoulder season amid mild weather forecasts with highs in the 60s-70s, curbing power sector demand. June 2026 futures trade near $2.78, implying limited upside absent hotter-than-normal temperatures. Traders eye Thursday's EIA Weekly Natural Gas Storage Report and updated weather models as key catalysts through month-end resolution.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump publicly insult Friedrich Merz by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump makes any public statement in which he insults, mocks, or attacks the listed individual personally or professionally in a clearly negative manner between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

This includes calling the individual weak, stupid, disloyal, a failure, using an insulting nickname, using other derogatory language, or using the negative form of a positive trait in a derogatory personal way (e.g., “He/She isn’t smart”). Negative forms used in reference to the individual's professional actions, policies, or decisions (e.g., “He/She isn’t being smart about this policy”) will not count. Policy disagreements stated without disparaging language will not count.

A direct reference will qualify even if the individual is not named, so long as it is reasonably clear from context that they are the subject.

Any written, verbal, or recorded public statement by Trump qualifies.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's frequent Truth Social posts and press exchanges have driven trader focus on his pattern of public insults, with recent escalations amid Iran tensions where he labeled critical media coverage "seditious" and personally assailed female reporters like ABC's Rachel Scott as "stupid" or "terrible." This follows April attacks on Pope Leo XIV as "weak" and "liberal," sparking Catholic backlash, alongside memes mocking Democrats such as Hakeem Jeffries and jabs at Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. Ongoing Gulf hostilities, including Iranian strikes on U.S. warships, heighten risks of further barbs at foreign leaders or domestic critics before the May 31 resolution, as White House briefings and diplomatic developments loom.

markets.question: Will Chicago Sky win the 2026 WNBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is determined as the champion of the WNBA for the 2026 season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to named as the champion of WNBA for the 2026 season per the rules of WNBA (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after November 14, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the WNBA: however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Liberty lead trader consensus at 30.5% implied probability for the 2026 WNBA title following their acquisition of three-time All-Star Satou Sabally in free agency, bolstering a core featuring Breanna Stewart and Sabrina Ionescu that reached the 2025 Finals. Atlanta Dream sit close at 25.5% after trading for Angel Reese at a bargain—ceding only future first-round picks—while retaining franchise-record 30-win contributors Allisha Gray and Rhyne Howard. Indiana Fever (18%) and defending champions Las Vegas Aces (16%) round out the tight top four, with Caitlin Clark-Kelsey Mitchell-Aliyah Boston health and A'ja Wilson's dynasty bid drawing bets amid league parity from the April draft, roster cuts finalized yesterday, and expansion to 15 teams diluting depth but elevating multiple contenders.

markets.question: Spread: AD Municipal Liberia (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Liga Promerica game, scheduled for May 10 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "AD Municipal Liberia" if AD Municipal Liberia win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Deportivo Saprissa".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on unafut.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Deportivo Saprissa vs. AD Municipal Liberia - More Markets

eventMetadata.context_description: Deportivo Saprissa, second in the Primera División Clausura standings with 31 points from 17 matches, host AD Municipal Liberia, who sit third on 30 points after a stunning 2-1 upset victory at Estadio Ricardo Saprissa Aymá on April 12—Liberia's first win there in 23 head-to-head meetings, ending Saprissa's unbeaten streak despite their prior 5-1 cup rout on March 29. Saprissa's recent form shows prolific scoring (10 goals in last five), but defensive lapses allowed late concessions, boosting Liberia's confidence amid the tight title race. No major injuries reported from official team updates; home advantage and historical dominance (19 wins, 7 draws, 2 losses overall) favor Saprissa rebound, though Liberia's away resilience adds upset potential for props like total goals (averaging 3.21 H2H).

If no 2026 Michigan Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Abdul El-Sayed at 52.5% implied probability to win Michigan's Democratic Senate primary on August 4, driven by his surging support among under-40 voters (35% vs. 18% for Mallory McMorrow in the April Emerson poll) and strong small-dollar fundraising that matched McMorrow while outpacing Haley Stevens in Q1 reports. Recent Emerson (April 11-13) and Glengariff (April 17-19) surveys show a tight race with high undecideds (36%), yet markets price in progressive momentum from El-Sayed's endorsements by Bernie Sanders and Rashida Tlaib amid intra-party clashes over Israel policy. Stevens gained from outgoing Sen. Debbie Stabenow's endorsement last week, lifting her to 16.1%, while McMorrow slips to 28% despite prior leads.

markets.question: Will the US acquire part of Greenland in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States acquires control of any land territory that is part of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the transfer of sovereignty, or the acquisition of primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control qualifies.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects an 86.5% implied probability against the United States acquiring any part of Greenland in 2026, driven by firm Danish and Greenlandic rejections of territorial transfer amid President Trump's renewed push for purchase or annexation. Since January 2026, Trump has floated $700 billion offers and tariff threats on European allies, while U.S. lawmakers like Sen. Ted Cruz and Rep. Fine introduced acquisition bills, citing Arctic security against Russia and China. However, Greenland's premier insists sovereignty is non-negotiable, and ongoing U.S.-Denmark-Greenland diplomatic talks—described as on a "good trajectory" in April—focus on expanding military access under the 1951 defense agreement and mineral rights, not ownership change. Legal barriers under international law and NATO cohesion further dampen prospects before year-end.

markets.question: Will NVIDIA (NVDA) hit (HIGH) $224 Week of May 4 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 4 2026, any 1-minute candle for NVIDIA (NVDA) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the NVIDIA (NVDA) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.NVDA%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.NVDA%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trump administration signals of mulling NATO withdrawal have intensified trader caution after European allies declined to join U.S. military operations amid the ongoing Iran conflict, prompting President Trump's public rebukes and threats as recently as early April 2026. Bipartisan congressional legislation from 2023 bars unilateral U.S. exit, requiring Senate approval, while incoming Secretary of State Marco Rubio previously sponsored such measures, adding procedural hurdles under NATO's Article 13 one-year notice provision. Lawmakers from both parties have pushed back forcefully, and no formal withdrawal initiation has occurred despite troop repositioning discussions. Traders price year-end exit odds at just 8%, reflecting skepticism over political barriers and alliance inertia, with NATO Secretary General Rutte's upcoming talks a potential catalyst.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a tight contest for Argentina's 2026 annual CPI inflation, with the 30.0-34.9% band leading at 37.8% implied probability ahead of 25-29.9% at 30.0%, reflecting recent central bank survey revisions to a 30.5% year-end consensus—up 1.4 percentage points amid March's 3.4% month-on-month print, the highest of 2026 and pushing year-to-date accumulation to 9.4%. Year-over-year inflation eased slightly to 32.6% in March from 33.2% prior, underscoring President Milei's fiscal austerity gains versus persistent services and regulated price pressures. Key swing factors include upcoming April INDEC data (due mid-May) and sustained monetary restraint; upside risks to 35%+ stem from potential wage inertia, while sub-25% requires sharper monthly deceleration.

events.title: MLS Cup Winner 2026
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 MLS Cup. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2026 MLS Cup per the rules of MLS (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 MLS season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Major League Soccer; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending MLS Cup champions Inter Miami CF lead trader consensus at 17.5% implied probability despite a third-place Eastern Conference standing (19 points from 11 games) and a shocking 4-3 home collapse against rivals Orlando City on May 2, blowing a 3-0 lead amid defensive lapses that dropped them in recent power rankings. Vancouver Whitecaps (25 points, +20 GD) and San Jose Earthquakes (28 points, Western leaders) dominate power rankings with unbeaten streaks and prolific attacks, boosting their 10.1% and 4.7% shares, while LAFC's steady third-place form (21 points) supports 11.5%. Nashville SC's Eastern table-topping 23 points adds to the bunching, as early-season volatility—around one-third through the 34-game schedule—keeps the playoff path to MLS Cup wide open amid shifting momentum.

markets.question: Will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit (HIGH) $7.00 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) shares fell over 6% to $5.01 on May 8, 2026, immediately after the May 7 Q1 earnings release showed $720 million in revenue—down 37% year-over-year—and a wider-than-expected $49 million net loss, despite revenue beats and management touting improved cohort margins and resale velocity. Persistent challenges include -30% profit margins, 131% debt-to-equity, and -151% return on equity, amplified by OPEN's high 3.66 beta amid a housing market with 6.15% 30-year mortgage rates and inventory rising toward 1.36 million homes, curbing iBuying volumes. Analyst consensus holds at "Hold" with a $4.86 average price target, as traders eye monthly housing data and potential Fed rate signals through May's end.

markets.question: Sabalenka vs. Cirstea: Match O/U 21.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Aryna Sabalenka and Sorana Cirstea in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 9, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 22. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Aryna Sabalenka vs Sorana Cirstea Match O/U 21.5

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Aryna Sabalenka vs Sorana Cirstea
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Aryna Sabalenka and Sorana Cirstea in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 9, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Aryna Sabalenka' if Aryna Sabalenka advances against Sorana Cirstea.

This market will resolve to 'Sorana Cirstea' if Sorana Cirstea advances against Aryna Sabalenka.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka enters as a heavy favorite at the Internazionali BNL d'Italia after a dominant 6-2, 6-3 second-round win over Barbora Krejcikova on clay, showcasing her explosive baseline power and improved movement on the surface following a strong Madrid run marred only by a quarterfinal loss to Hailey Baptiste. Sorana Cirstea, ranked around No. 26 and on her farewell 2026 tour, advanced comfortably 6-2, 6-0 over Tatjana Maria despite a recent leg injury withdrawal in Rouen, but trails 2-1 in head-to-heads, including a straight-sets defeat to Sabalenka in Brisbane earlier this year. Trader consensus reflects Sabalenka's ranking edge, youth, and stylistic matchup superiority on Rome's slower clay, though Cirstea's veteran experience could extend rallies.

eventMetadata.context_description: Slavia Praha's status as Czech First League leaders with an eight-point advantage over second-placed Sparta Praha, combined with home advantage at Fortuna Arena, underpins trader consensus pricing Slavia at 53.5% implied probability in this pivotal Prague Derby. Slavia's recent form shows four wins in their last six matches, including a dominant 3-1 victory over Sparta in March 2026, extending their unbeaten run in the last three head-to-heads (two wins, one draw). Sparta remains competitive with four wins in six but trails in the table and recent derbies, elevating draw odds to 27% in this historically tight rivalry averaging 2.3 goals per match. Defensive concerns for Slavia, including absences like Jhoanner Chávez, temper the favorite's edge without shifting the closely contested sentiment.

markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold (GC) June futures hover near $4,730 per ounce as of May 8, 2026, reflecting trader consensus on sustained bullish drivers amid a 2% weekly gain following a two-week pullback. Robust central bank demand—244 tonnes purchased in Q1 alone, led by emerging markets—bolsters reserves diversification, while sticky inflation (March CPI at 3.3% year-over-year) and the Federal Reserve's steady 3.5%-3.75% federal funds rate suppress real yields, favoring the non-yielding metal. A softer U.S. dollar and muted Middle East risk premium add support. Key catalysts include the May CPI release and June FOMC meeting, where rate cut signals could accelerate upside toward $5,000 or trigger reversals if yields spike.

markets.question: Will Jean Castex be on the ballot for the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held in April 2027. Prior to the election, the French Constitutional Council is expected to publish the official list of candidates to be included on the ballot for the first round of this election.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed individual is included on the official candidate list for the first round of the 2027 French presidential election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no such candidate list is published prior to the election, this market will resolve based on which candidates actually appear on the ballot when the election takes place. If no list is published and the specified election does not take place by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official candidate list published by the French Constitutional Council; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With President Emmanuel Macron term-limited after two consecutive terms, the 2027 French presidential election—first round expected mid-April—features a fragmented field requiring 500 parrainages from elected officials for ballot access. Recent declarations include hard-left leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon's May 3 entry for La France Insoumise, Les Républicains' Bruno Retailleau (official nominee April 19), and centrist Édouard Philippe, alongside fringe figures like Nicolas Dupont-Aignan. Polls show undeclared National Rally head Jordan Bardella leading first-round intentions at 35%, reflecting post-2024 hung parliament instability and municipal election dynamics where the far right gained nationally despite local setbacks. Key upcoming events: Marine Le Pen's July 7 ineligibility appeal and a United Left primary on October 11 could reshape contenders.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Europe at 71.5% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, driven by UEFA's unmatched depth across 16 qualifiers including powerhouses France, Spain, England, Germany, and Portugal, bolstered by recent European Qualifiers success and strong Nations League form. South America's 20.5% reflects CONMEBOL's six direct qualifiers like defending champions Argentina, Brazil, and Uruguay, whose stylistic edge and head-to-head history keep them viable despite fewer slots. Lower odds for Africa (CAF's nine teams, led by Morocco), Asia (AFC's eight), North America (CONCACAF hosts USA, Canada, Mexico plus three), and Oceania stem from historical barriers and lack of prior winners, unchanged since March 2026 inter-confederation playoffs finalized the 48-team field with no major upsets shifting sentiment.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Istanbul be 20°C on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by NOAA at the Istanbul Airport in degrees Celsius on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=LTFM

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors 22°C at 34% implied probability for Istanbul's highest temperature on May 9, closely trailed by 21°C (25%) and 23°C (21%), reflecting tight clustering in latest forecast models amid inherent short-term uncertainty. The European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) and Global Forecast System (GFS) ensembles project afternoon maxima around 22°C under a building high-pressure ridge over the eastern Mediterranean, fostering mild advection from the Black Sea; however, discrepancies arise from variable low-level moisture and cloud cover, potentially capping peaks at 21°C or allowing slight diurnal heating to 23°C via urban heat island effects in the city center. Typical May climatology averages 21°C highs, with Turkish State Meteorological Service updates expected through the day to refine trajectories before resolution based on official station observations.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol remains in custody at the Seoul Detention Center following a February 2026 life imprisonment sentence for leading an insurrection tied to his December 2024 martial law declaration, upheld amid ongoing appeals. An April 29 appeals court ruling increased his term to seven years for resisting arrest and bypassing a legitimate Cabinet meeting, reinforcing trader consensus at 92.5% for "No" on release before 2027. Multiple convictions, including a prior five-year obstruction sentence, face Supreme Court review, but procedural delays and political fallout from his impeachment and removal diminish prospects for near-term freedom, barring extraordinary pardon or reversal. Recent related rulings, like ex-Prime Minister Han Duck-soo's reduced 15-year term on May 7, highlight judicial scrutiny without altering Yoon's detention.

markets.question: Will the US confirm that aliens exist before Kevin Warsh is confirmed as Fed Chair?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the President of the United States, any member of the Cabinet of the United States, any member of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, or any US federal agency definitively states that extraterrestrial life or technology exists before Kevin Warsh is confirmed as chair of the Federal Reserve. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

Kevin Warsh being confirmed as chair of the Federal Reserve requires the Senate to confirm Warsh’s nomination as Chair of the Federal Reserve. Recess appointments without Senate confirmation will not count. Senate confirmation of Warsh as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the U.S. Senate and the government of the United States, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: What will happen before Kevin Warsh is confirmed?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran before Kevin Warsh is confirmed as chair of the Federal Reserve. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

Kevin Warsh being confirmed as chair of the Federal Reserve requires the Senate to confirm Warsh’s nomination as Chair of the Federal Reserve. Recess appointments without Senate confirmation will not count. Senate confirmation of Warsh as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another.

If the agreement is officially reached before Kevin Warsh is confirmed as Fed Chair, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

The primary resolution sources for this market are official information from the U.S. Senate and official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, a wide consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached or a consensus of credible reporting that Warsh has been confirmed will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kevin Warsh's nomination to serve as Federal Reserve chair advanced through the Senate Banking Committee on April 29, 2026, via a 13-11 party-line vote following his April 21 confirmation hearing, where Democrats pressed him on Fed independence and past Trump ties but garnered no support. The process cleared a prior Republican hold by Sen. Thom Tillis, positioning the nomination for a full Senate floor vote, potentially the week of May 11, ahead of Jerome Powell's term expiration on May 15. Traders weigh Republican majority math favoring confirmation against Democratic opposition and any last-minute holds, with no reported withdrawals or rejections to date.

eventMetadata.context_description: Auckland FC hosts Adelaide United in the A-League Men playoffs semifinal first leg at Go Media Stadium on May 9, with the visitors holding second-place momentum from the regular season while Auckland advanced via a gritty 1-1 penalty shootout win over Melbourne City in their elimination final. Trader consensus reflects Auckland's home advantage and competitive head-to-head history—featuring recent 1-1 and 2-2 draws—but tempers it with the hosts' injury crisis: Golden Boot co-winner Sam Cosgrove (ankle ligaments) and Jesse Randall are major doubts, alongside lingering concerns for Jake Brimmer (calf) and goalkeeper Michael Woud (groin). Adelaide enters healthier, riding a strong finish that saw them stun Melbourne City late in the campaign, setting up a tight aggregate contest ahead of the May 15 return leg.

markets.question: Will Putin visit China by May 31?
markets.description:
If Vladimir Putin visits China by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Putin physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of China. Whether or not Putin enters Chinese airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on this market's resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from Vladimir Putin, the Federal Government of Russia, and the Chinese government; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kremlin aide Yuri Ushakov stated on May 7 that Moscow and Beijing will jointly announce dates for President Vladimir Putin's visit to China, deeming unilateral disclosure "unfriendly," signaling advanced bilateral diplomacy. This follows Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's April 15 confirmation of a trip in the first half of 2026, with Russian media citing preliminary late-May scheduling amid deepening strategic partnership. Putin accepted Xi Jinping's invitation during their February videoconference, with Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov noting active preparations. Traders price Yes at 76% reflecting these official commitments and historical reliability of Russia-China summits, though final dates remain pending amid U.S.-China talks potentially influencing timing.

markets.question: Solana Up or Down on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT May 8 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is lower than the final "Close" price for the May 9 '26 12:00 ET candle.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT May 8 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is higher than the final "Close" price for the May 9 '26 12:00 ET candle.

If the final "Close" price for both of these candles is exactly equal on Binance, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat April McClain-Delaney's strong position drives trader consensus favoring the Democratic Party at 87.5% implied probability to win Maryland's 6th Congressional District House seat in November 2026. The district, blending Republican-leaning western Maryland areas with Democratic Montgomery County suburbs, leans D+3 per partisan indexes, where she secured a narrow 2024 victory over Republican Neil Parrott. Recent polls show Delaney leading challenger David Trone 49%-38% ahead of the June 23 Democratic primary, bolstered by her 80% approval amid escalating campaign attacks including a cease-and-desist letter. The Republican primary features only three lesser-known candidates, lacking a standout contender, reinforcing Democratic structural advantages despite the GOP's slim current House majority.

markets.question: Will inflation reach more than 5% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index reports released for each month of 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm). Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the December 2026 report is issued. Once the December 2026 report is issued, any revisions to previously released CPI figures will not be counted toward this market's resolution. If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on 2026 peak inflation hinges on March's Consumer Price Index surging to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024 and up sharply from February's 2.4%—driven primarily by soaring energy prices, including a 10.9% monthly gasoline spike. This reacceleration has amplified concerns over potential 4%+ monthly readings in coming months, as flagged by analysts citing relentless oil advances, though core CPI rose more modestly at 2.6% annually. Market-implied odds reflect caution, with 10-year Treasury breakeven inflation rates steady near 2.5% as of early May, aligning with Federal Reserve March projections for PCE inflation around 2.5% for the year amid sticky services pressures. The April CPI release on May 12 will be pivotal, alongside June FOMC deliberations, as labor resilience and commodity volatility shape the trajectory toward year-end highs.

markets.question: Will François Hollande win the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices National Rally leader Jordan Bardella at 24.5% to win France's 2027 presidential election, driven by his dominant first-round showings in recent polls like Harris Interactive's late April survey (35%) and Ifop's May wish-to-run data (44%), fueled by ongoing discontent with the hung parliament and post-2024 snap election fragmentation. Former Prime Minister Édouard Philippe trails at 18.5%, bolstered by Elabe polling showing him edging Bardella in runoffs (51.5-48.5%), while Jean-Luc Mélenchon's May 3 candidacy announcement lifts his left-wing prospects to 8.5%. The race stays tight amid a crowded field of over 30 names and no party primaries yet, with Marine Le Pen's pending appeal verdict capping her at 5.5%. Separation could emerge from RN nominee selection favoring Bardella, left coalition negotiations, or centrist consolidation around Philippe before the April 2027 first round.

markets.question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (LOW) $40 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or below the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil June 2026 futures trade near $96/bbl, reflecting trader consensus on Polymarket and futures markets amid heightened Middle East geopolitical risks, including Strait of Hormuz tensions and Iran ceasefire uncertainty, which have driven a 1.2% spot price rebound to $95.92 on May 8 despite a 2% monthly decline. Fundamentals remain bearish, with EIA reporting a smaller-than-expected 2.3 million barrel inventory draw to 457 million barrels for the week ending May 1, elevated global stocks, and forecasts of Q2 Brent peaks near $115 before surpluses push prices below $90 by year-end. Key catalysts include weekly EIA reports, the June 7 OPEC+ ministerial meeting on production quotas, and impending U.S. summer driving season demand.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve held the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% in its April 29, 2026 meeting—the third consecutive pause—amid reaccelerating inflation, with March CPI at 3.3% year-over-year, the highest since May 2024, and robust nonfarm payrolls adding 178,000 jobs. This resilience has tempered immediate rate cut prospects, contrasting the March dot plot's median projection of 3.00%-3.25% by end-2027 amid cooling growth forecasts. Polymarket traders, committing over $1.4 million in volume, reflect skin-in-the-game consensus pricing a rate dip below current levels before December 31, 2026, per official FOMC bounds. Critical catalysts include April CPI on May 12, labor data, and the June 16-17 FOMC, where data-dependent signals could reshape the policy path.

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Rebeka Masarova vs Leylah Fernandez
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Rebeka Masarova and Leylah Fernandez in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 8, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Rebeka Masarova' if Rebeka Masarova advances against Leylah Fernandez.

This market will resolve to 'Leylah Fernandez' if Leylah Fernandez advances against Rebeka Masarova.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: No. 25 seed Leylah Fernandez holds a significant ranking edge over qualifier Rebeka Masarova (No. 160) in their first head-to-head clash on the clay courts of the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, a WTA 1000 event, fueling trader consensus toward the Canadian left-hander's defensive prowess and experience. Masarova's momentum from three gritty qualifying wins—including straight-sets over Moyuka Uchijima and a come-from-behind victory against Oksana Selekhmeteva in the round of 128—tests Fernandez, who enters off a Madrid quarterfinal run lost to Mirra Andreeva despite a 9-11 season record. Masarova's potential fatigue from short turnaround contrasts Fernandez's fresher legs, with break point efficiency and service holds key on the slower Roman clay.

markets.question: Will G2 Esports qualify to MSI 2026?
markets.description:
The MSI 2026 (Mid-Season Invitational) is scheduled to take place from June 26 to July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With MSI 2026 qualifiers looming via June regional playoffs and Road to MSI tournaments, trader consensus hinges on Split 2 regular season finales across major regions, where frontrunners hold prime positioning for top seeds. In LCK, Hanwha Life Esports (10W-1L) and KT Rolster (9W-2L) lead after extending win streaks in recent matchdays, edging Gen.G and T1 for advantageous playoff paths. LEC sees Team Vitality (8W-1L) and undefeated Karmine Corp atop standings, while LPL's LGD Gaming and LNG Esports share early Group Stage leads at 3-1. LCP's Team Secret Whales dominate at 5-0, and LTA North's FlyQuest paces NA. Recent roster tweaks and patch meta shifts have fueled momentum swings, with no spots locked as playoffs approach.

markets.question: Will Trump speak to Elon Musk in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual speaks with Donald Trump between May 1 and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Speaking is defined as any verbal interaction between the listed individual and Donald Trump, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

The resolution source will be credible media reporting. However, in the absence of definitive or in the case of contradictory media reporting, statements by either of the specified individuals or their official representatives will also be considered.

Statements by the specified individuals or their official representatives will only be considered conclusive if the claims explicitly, or in context unambiguously, indicate that a qualifying talk occurred within the specified timeframe and took place via verbal communication, and provided that such claims are not contradicted by the other specified individual, their representatives, or credible media reporting by the end of the third calendar date (ET) following the statement in question.

If the date/time of a qualifying talk cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day following this market's above-specified timeframe, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether it was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Donald Trump's confirmed summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on May 14-15 drives primary trader focus, following White House announcements of the trip—postponed from April amid U.S.-Iran naval exchanges in the Strait of Hormuz and fragile ceasefire negotiations. Trump reaffirmed anticipation during May 4 remarks, highlighting talks on trade tariffs, AI competition, and regional stability. No verbal interactions with other listed figures like French President Emmanuel Macron, NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, or Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy have been reported in May so far, though Ukraine aid dynamics, NATO consultations, and European tariff pressures could prompt unscheduled phone or video calls before the May 31 resolution. Yesterday's bilateral meeting with Brazil's President Lula da Silva does not qualify under market rules.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump's tie be blue on May 9, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on the predominant color of the tie worn by Donald Trump during his first public appearance during which he wears a tie on the specified date (ET).

The color will be determined based solely on the first publicly available photo or video of the appearance. Reporting of the tie color without photos or videos will not be considered. Subsequent publication of other photos or videos, including those taken earlier, will not affect the resolution.

For the tie to be considered predominantly blue or red, its primary color must be a shade of blue or red, though it may feature other colors as part of a pattern. 

This tie would count as "Blue": 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/donald-trump-tie-color-blue-5ywRT5OXfn-5.jpg

This tie would count as "Red": 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/donald-trump-tie-color-red-a7ObxtpGQcrR.jpg

The following tie has no distinguishable predominant color, and thus would resolve to "Other": 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/donald-trump-tie-color-other-Q8aIgOP7wFs1.jpg

If Donald Trump does not have any public appearance on the specified date, does not wear a tie, or if it is a color not listed among the strike options, the market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Anthropic be acquired before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if credible reporting confirms that any entity enters into an agreement to acquire the listed company by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Mergers where the listed company is subsumed by another entity will count toward a "Yes" resolution.

An announced agreement between the listed company and an acquiring entity will qualify for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of whether the acquisition is ultimately completed.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the listed company and/or its leadership; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment in the tech-focused acquisition market hinges on surging AI consolidation, exemplified by SpaceX's April 2026 announcement securing a $60 billion option to acquire AI coding startup Cursor—building on Microsoft's prior interest and fueling expectations of a full deal before year-end. This reflects Big Tech's aggressive pursuit of specialized AI capabilities like code-generation tools amid booming M&A activity driven by the AI race. Other contenders like Perplexity AI face speculation over search engine integrations, while independents such as OpenAI and Anthropic trade low due to ample funding and strategic autonomy. Key catalysts include Q2 earnings calls, developer conferences like TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, and regulatory reviews, with resolution tied to announced agreements by December 31.

markets.question: Will Benoni Mendes win the 2026 Minas Gerais gubernatorial election?
markets.description:
The Minas Gerais gubernatorial election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, with a runoff on October 25, 2026, if no candidate receives a majority of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder governors appointed by any means before the specified election will not be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Senator Cleitinho Azevedo (Republicanos) at 56.5% implied probability to win the October 4, 2026, Minas Gerais gubernatorial election, driven by consistent first-round leads and second-round advantages in late-April Genial/Quaest and Quaest polls, where he topped scenarios against rivals like Rodrigo Pacheco, Alexandre Kalil, and Mateus Simões by 18–33 points. These surveys, conducted April 22–26, reflect his strong appeal among right-leaning voters amid incumbent Romeu Zema's earlier presidential bid resignation, elevating Simões as acting governor. Pacheco's candidacy decision looms by late May, while fragmented opposition and no fresh polls sustain Azevedo's frontrunner status in this two-round race, though alliances or scandals could shift dynamics ahead of primaries.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries' unchallenged position as the Democratic nominee in solidly Democratic NY-08 (D+24 partisan voting index) underpins trader consensus favoring a Democratic House winner at 92%. Recent withdrawals of primary challengers, including councilmember Chi Osse after lacking key endorsements, leave Jeffries unopposed ahead of the June 23 closed primary, bolstered by $5.4 million cash on hand. Weak Republican contenders Lewis Mizrahi and Richard Simmons show no fundraising, aligning with Cook Political Report's Solid Democratic rating and Jeffries' past general election margins exceeding 75%. While a surprise GOP primary upset, personal scandal, or national Republican midterm surge could narrow odds, structural district advantages and incumbency present high barriers.

markets.question: Will Rizo Velovic win Survivor Season 50?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the contestant who wins Survivor season 50.

If Survivor season 50 concludes without a winner being declared, or if Survivor season 50 has otherwise not concluded by June 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET this market will resolve to "Other". In the case of a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed contestant whose name comes first in alphabetical order. 

This market will remain open until the conclusion of the season.

The resolution source for this market will be the official broadcast of the final episode of Survivor season 50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Aubry Bracco commands an overwhelming 87.5% implied probability as the Survivor 50 frontrunner, propelled by her pivotal role in Episode 11's double-elimination chaos, where she navigated a soul-crushing blindside to oust Ozzy Lusth—idol in pocket—and Emily Flippen, solidifying her jury positioning in this all-legends season. Traders view her adaptive gameplay, blending subtle strategy with alliance control, as echoing past returnee winners like Cirie Fields, who trails at 7.2% as a social threat eyeing an upset. Jonathan Young and Joe Hunter linger at 2.5% and 2.4% on challenge strength. With the May 20 finale looming, final immunity wins and fire-making could shift jury sentiment in unpredictable ways.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Chicago be between 48-49°F on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/il/chicago/KORD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service forecasts for Chicago O'Hare International Airport project a high temperature near 65-67°F on May 9, driven by a warming trend from southerly winds and high pressure building over the Midwest following a cool early May with highs in the upper 50s. Model consensus from NOAA ensembles shows minimal uncertainty, with partly sunny skies and low precipitation odds (under 20%) supporting daytime heating above the 68°F climatological normal. Trader consensus at 99.7% implied probability for 56°F or higher reflects this robust outlook backed by real capital. Realistic challenges include an unexpected late cold front or persistent low clouds capping temperatures below 56°F, though upper-air patterns make this unlikely; monitor afternoon NWS updates for final guidance.

markets.question: Will Estonia be in the top 3 at Eurovision 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the named country finishes Eurovision 2026 as one of the top 3 highest scoring candidates.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to finish as one of the top 3 highest scorers based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.
